trade.summary: Growing bilateral trade. Switzerland exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, and chemicals. Poland exports machinery, furniture, food products, and automotive components. A large Polish diaspora in Switzerland facilitates people-to-people links.

diplomatic.summary: Friendly relations. Both countries support democratic values and a rules-based international order. Poland appreciates Switzerland's financial sector and role as a neutral mediator. Switzerland has contributed to EU cohesion funds that benefit Poland (Swiss Contribution). Coordination on Ukraine/Russia issues.

military.summary: Switzerland is neutral; Poland is a NATO member focused on eastern flank security. No formal military cooperation. Switzerland has provided humanitarian support related to Russia-Ukraine conflict.

financial.summary: Swiss companies are major investors in Poland, particularly in pharmaceuticals, retail, and manufacturing. Swiss banks provide financial services to Polish companies. Growing bilateral investment as Poland's economy develops.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Switzerland supports Poland's energy transition through investment. Both connected through European energy networks.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. ETH Zurich and Swiss universities collaborate with Polish institutions through Horizon Europe. Joint publications in physics, chemistry, and IT are increasing.
